Check whether the proof conditions at each step are sufficient.For reasoning steps<br/> that have gaps or jumps in logic, you should request lemmas to bridge these gaps.you should provide the formal statement of the lemma in <br/>Isabelle (without proving the lemma).</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b><br/>## Function definitions</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>typedef </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">(overloaded) &prime;a bstack =<br/>&quot;{xs :: (&prime;a list × nat). length (fst xs) ≤ snd xs}&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">morphisms </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">alist_of Abs_bstack<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>proof</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> -<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>have</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> &quot;([],0) ∈ {xs. length (fst xs) ≤ snd xs}&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>by </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> capacity :: &quot;&prime;a bstack ⇒ nat&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>where </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#0099ff;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">capacity </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">s ≡ snd (alist_of s)&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>definition </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">size :: &quot;&prime;a bstack ⇒ nat&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">isempty :: &quot;&prime;a bstack ⇒ bool&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">s ≡ fst (alist_of s) = []&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> push :: &quot;&prime;a ⇒ &prime;a bstack ⇒ &prime;a bstack&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>where </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">v s ≡ (if ¬isfull s then Abs_bstack (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s)) else s)&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>definition</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> pop :: &quot;&prime;a bstack ⇒ (&prime;a option × &prime;a bstack)&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>where </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">s ≡ (if ¬isempty s <br/> then (Some (hd (fst (alist_of s))), Abs_bstack (tl (fst (alist_of s)), snd 	(alist_of s))) else (None, s))&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>definition</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> top :: &quot;&prime;a bstack ⇒ &prime;a option&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>where </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">	&quot;</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#0099ff;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">top </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">s ≡ (if ¬isempty s then (Some (hd (fst (alist_of s)))) else None)&quot;<br/><br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>## Theorem statement</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/>```isabelle<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>theorem</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> bounded_stack_push_updates_top:<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>assumes </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">a1:&quot;¬ isfull s&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>shows </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;top (push v s) = Some v&quot;<br/>```<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>## Informal proof </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/>To prove the theorem push_updates_top, we need to proceed step by step, ensuring that each reasoning step is as small as possible .<br/>Step1: Expanding the Definition of push:<br/>	The first step in the proof is to expand the definition of push given the assumption \&lt;not&gt; isfull s. According to the definition of push, since the ..<br/>Step2:Proving Non-Empty Stack After Push:<br/>	After pushing the element v onto s, we need to establish that the resulting stack is not empty. This is a necessary step before we can use the ...<br/>Step3: Applying the Definition of top:<br/>	Given that the stack is not empty after the push operation, we can now apply the definition of top. The top function, when applied to a non-empty ..<br/>	We proceed by relating the alist_of representation of the stack after the push operation. Specifically, we establish that alist_of (push v s) is equal...<br/>Step5: Simplifying the hd Expression:<br/>	With the result from the previous step, we can now simplify the expression for hd (fst (alist_of (push v s))) to obtain v, as it is the head of the ...<br/>Step6: Conclusion: Finally, combining the results from steps 3 and 5, we conclude that top (push v s) is indeed equal to Some v.<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>## Required new lemma</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/>Thought1: Step 4: Relating alist_of with Abs_bstack : `... = (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))`. Explanation: This step simplifies the expression ..<br/>### new lemma1<br/>```isabelle<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>lemma</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> alist_of_Abs_bstack_hd_tl: </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>assumes </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;length (fst xs) ≤ snd xs&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>shows </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;alist_of (Abs_bstack xs) = xs&quot;<br/>```<br/>Thought2: In your proof of bounded_stack_push_updates_top, the reasoning gap in step 5 can indeed be resolved by introducing a formal lemma...<br/>### new lemma2<br/>```isabelle<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>lemma </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">alist_of_Abs_bstack_push: </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>assumes </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;length (fst (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))) ≤ snd (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>shows </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;alist_of (Abs_bstack (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))) = (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s))&quot;<br/>```</font></p></div>" tags="Background" id="sixPqVdEzeB0jIY1dAgv-12"> <mxCell parent="1" style="verticalAlign=middle;align=left;overflow=width;vsdxID=2;fillColor=none;gradientColor=none;shape=stencil(nZBRDoQgDERP03+WHsHFe5iISyOCQdT19kIaIxq//Jtp3zTpAFaTaUYNUkwx+F6v1EYD+AUpyRkdKCYFqACrzgf9C352LfuxyWRWg1/yhT/nRE5IsZWuZtCSK8CPeI0e/hF+jV7AJM4PO7KWCyr390bSiNtEtQM=);strokeColor=none;spacingTop=-3;spacingBottom=-3;spacingLeft=-3;spacingRight=-3;points=[];labelBackgroundColor=none;rounded=0;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="710" width="660" x="567" y="665" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </UserObject> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 662px; height: 1px; padding-top: 375px; margin-left: 10px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; width: 660px; max-height: 710px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; width: 100%; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

font.style: font-size: 14.11px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font:
You are an expert in constructing lemmas from reasoning and analysis.Your task is to analyze the informal proof of this theorem, examining
each reasoning step for rules that are unclear or ambiguous. Check whether the proof conditions at each step are sufficient.For reasoning steps
that have gaps or jumps in logic, you should request lemmas to bridge these gaps.you should provide the formal statement of the lemma in
Isabelle (without proving the lemma).

p.font:
(overloaded) ′a bstack =
"{xs :: (′a list × nat). length (fst xs) ≤ snd xs}"

p.font: v s ≡ (if ¬isfull s then Abs_bstack (v # fst (alist_of s), snd (alist_of s)) else s)"

s ≡ (if ¬isempty s

Step1: Expanding the Definition of push:
The first step in the proof is to expand the definition of push given the assumption \<not> isfull s. According to the definition of push, since the ..
Step2:Proving Non-Empty Stack After Push:
After pushing the element v onto s, we need to establish that the resulting stack is not empty. This is a necessary step before we can use the ...
Step3: Applying the Definition of top:
Given that the stack is not empty after the push operation, we can now apply the definition of top. The top function, when applied to a non-empty ..
We proceed by relating the alist_of representation of the stack after the push operation. Specifically, we establish that alist_of (push v s) is equal...
Step5: Simplifying the hd Expression:
With the result from the previous step, we can now simplify the expression for hd (fst (alist_of (push v s))) to obtain v, as it is the head of the ...
Step6: Conclusion: Finally, combining the results from steps 3 and 5, we conclude that top (push v s) is indeed equal to Some v.
